engineering.translation: Tatra taṁ buddhi-saṁyogaṁ labhate paurva-dehikam — there, in the new birth, the practitioner regains the union-of-intellect from the prior body, and from there strives further toward perfection. The verse is multi-life in its native register. Shankara: he becomes endowed with the wisdom acquired in the previous body. Ramanuja: he regains the mental disposition for Yoga, like one awakened from sleep, and resumes from where he had left off. The metaphysics exceeds engineering reach; the structural claim does not. The structural claim is that prior practice is not lost across a discontinuity. The substrate persists. The engineering analog operates at single-life scope. The engineer who shifted into management for three years and returned, the engineer who took the parental-leave gap, the engineer whose two startups failed and who came back at thirty-seven — none start from zero. The buddhi-saṁyoga from the prior body, scoped operationally, is the accumulated taste for the right abstraction, the calibrated instinct for which migration is risky. STRETCHED.
engineering.concrete_scenario: An engineer left full-time coding three years ago for a director-of-engineering role. She managed twenty people, sat in roadmap meetings, reviewed PRs without writing them, and watched her own commit history go quiet on GitHub. Six months ago she returned to an IC role at a different company, joining as principal on a Postgres-to-CockroachDB migration. The first week she felt slow — her fingers reached for IDE shortcuts that no longer worked, her mental cache of pg internals was stale, her terraform muscle had atrophied. By week three, the substrate began re-attaching. She found the connection-pool exhaustion bug the team had chased for two months in forty-five minutes — not because she remembered the specific fix, but because the *shape* of how a senior engineer reads a Datadog flamegraph had not been lost. The instinct for which abstraction would survive the migration and which one was implementation-leakage was still there. By month four she was operating at her prior level, with three years of management-context layered on top. The prior practice carried her, in Shankara's phrase, like a powerful habit. The metaphysics of multi-life rebirth exceeds this analog; the structural rung — substrate persists across discontinuity — is what the verse, scoped operationally, can describe.
engineering.falsifiability: The analog fails if a reader treats it as a guarantee that any career-gap returns automatically to prior level. The verse does not promise that. It claims the substrate persists; it does not claim the gap is costless. An engineer who reads this verse as 'time off doesn't matter' has misread it. The substrate is a ground-state advantage; the climb back to operating-level still demands the striving (yatate ca tato bhūyaḥ) the verse names alongside the substrate-claim.
engineering.counter_example: When the gap was genuine drift — three years not of management or family but of attention-fragmentation, social-media compulsion, no deep practice anywhere — the substrate has degraded. The verse names continued tendency from genuine prior practice; it does not endorse the recovery of substrate that was never deeply built or that was actively eroded. An engineer who hopped between four shallow codebases over five years and now expects buddhi-saṁyoga to carry her has not earned the verse's claim.
engineering.implication: If you have left the practice for a season and returned, the prior years are still operating in you. Allow the substrate to surface. Do not mistake the first-week slowness for first-week-zero.
engineering.quotable_line: The substrate from the prior body persists across the discontinuity; the climb back is from accumulated ground, not from zero.

iterations.mutation: v0 generation: BG 6.43 drafted from triangulated source pack. Per chapter-6 thesis flag (multi-life metaphysics is STRETCHED), engineering analog scoped explicitly to single-life-substrate (engineer returning from a three-year management gap). Translation honors Shankara (wisdom-from-prior-body) and Ramanuja (mental-disposition-for-Yoga, like one awakened from sleep). Confidence MEDIUM (not HIGH) because the verse's metaphysical reach exceeds the operational analog. Falsifiability and counter-example explicitly guard against the misread that the analog promises costless return. Tools: Postgres, CockroachDB, GitHub, Datadog, terraform.
